steps.title: Identify what the student's claim requires
steps.content: The student claims cultured meat can respond to "an expected widespread change in the public's behavior." The supporting quotation must identify what that behavioral change is — specifically, a shift in how people consume meat that creates rising demand.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — '"Consumers tend to believe that using less packaging when selling meat products in stores would have a significant effect on the environment."': Discusses consumer beliefs about packaging, not a behavioral change in meat consumption. ✗
  • Choice B — '"The flavor of meat from livestock differs across species (from pig to chicken to cow), and is also influenced by farming conditions and the breeds and genders of animals."': Describes flavor variation, not a shift in demand or consumption behavior. ✗
  • Choice C — '"A growing population that is increasingly consuming more meat in its diet will contribute to an increasing demand for meat products in the 21st century."': Directly identifies the behavioral change — a population consuming more meat — which is exactly the demand shift cultured meat responds to. ✓
  • Choice D — '"Researchers who advocate for the development of lab-grown meat claim that it's better for the environment than conventional meat because it requires less water consumption."': Discusses environmental benefits, not a change in public behavior that creates demand. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
